title: Public constant members should be avoided
description: Constant members are copied at compile time to the call sites, instead of being fetched at runtime. As an example, say you have a library with a constant Version member set to 1.0, and a client application linked to it. This library is then updated and Version is set to 2.0.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

logic.detection_conditions: Identifies public constant field declarations using regex pattern for 'public const'
exclusion_conditions: Static readonly fields (already following best practice)
logic.reasoning: Detects public constant field declarations which can cause versioning issues because constants are compiled into calling assemblies at compile time. When the constant value changes in a library, client code must be recompiled to see the new value. The regex pattern specifically looks for 'public const' but excludes private, internal, and protected constants which don't have this cross-assembly issue.
